author: Anup Chavan
description: Sync Apple services with Obsidian - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Anup Chavan
manifest.description: Sync Apple Journal entries with your vault.
manifest.name: Markway

name: Markway

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This 0.1.4 refresh prevents template files, provisional Obsidian note names, and offline bridge retries from creating unwanted Journal entries.

## Fixed
- Skip files in Obsidian’s configured Templates folder during Markway sync, using the persisted Templates config through the public vault adapter instead of private Obsidian APIs.
- Avoid pushing unique-note files while the configured note-name template still has an empty title slot, so provisional names like a timestamp-only note do not create duplicate Journal entries.
- Coalesce create/modify/rename push events so a note being renamed is pushed once at its settled path.
- Recover Journal push responses that complete while the plugin is disabled, so enabling the plugin links the original note instead of pushing the same file again.
- Keep pending Journal pushes out of startup scans and file watchers until Markway.app replies.
- When delete syncing is enabled, treat a missing linked Journal entry as deleted instead of recreating it on the next Obsidian edit.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release adds Journal media/template metadata sync, fixes created-date and path stability issues, and hardens the local Markway bridge.

## Added
- Added a configurable push-only Journal created-date frontmatter property.
- Added note-name template recovery for date-only frontmatter plus times embedded in filenames, including sanitized separators.
- Added Journal photo, video, and live photo support through `{{photos}}`, with generic attachments available through `{{attachments}}`.
- Added `{{reflection}}`, `{{places}}`, and motion activity metadata for Journal templates.
- Added a configurable photos frontmatter property that syncs exported attachment wikilinks back to Journal.
- Added `npm run watch:created-sync` for created-date and link-state diagnostics.

## Fixed
- Existing linked notes now keep their current vault path and filename during Journal pulls; the note-name template applies only to first import.
- Previously imported entries can self-repair Journal created metadata when the note-derived created date differs from Journal.
- Template/frontmatter output refreshes when Journal attachment metadata changes without body text changes.
- Empty generated template properties are skipped instead of adding blank frontmatter.
- Rules UI add/delete filter controls, default filter groups, duplicate condition normalization, and refresh behavior were fixed.
- Trimmed trailing newlines before pushing body text to Journal to avoid accumulating blank lines.
- Hardened bridge response validation against mismatched response IDs.
- Sanitized generated note filenames and rule/template folders so backslash, `.`, and `..` segments cannot move notes unexpectedly.

## Compatibility
- Requires Obsidian `1.6.6` or newer.
- Pair with Markway app `0.1.3` for matching Journal created-date metadata repairs and bridge hardening.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release fixes the Obsidian review warning about direct filesystem access.

## Changed
- Removed runtime use of Node.js `fs`, `fs/promises`, and `fs.watch` from the Obsidian plugin bridge.
- Moved bridge requests, responses, and events to the vault-local plugin bridge at `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/markway/bridge`.
- The plugin now writes bridge files through Obsidian’s vault adapter instead of direct filesystem APIs.
- Updated the README security model to match the implementation.
- Bumped plugin metadata to `0.1.2`.

## Compatibility
- Requires Markway 0.1.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Initial beta release of the Markway Obsidian plugin.

## What’s included

- Sync selected Markdown notes with Apple Journal through the native Markway app.
- Push the current note to Apple Journal.
- Pull Journal entries into Markdown.
- Sync Journal entries manually with a command.
- Automatic sync support for eligible files.
- Rule-based file filtering for deciding which notes sync with Journal.
- Journal folder setting and delete propagation options.
- Template-driven frontmatter generation.
- Music attachment frontmatter rendering via template variables.
- Sync metadata stored in plugin data instead of note frontmatter.
- Support for Obsidian’s legacy settings UI and newer declarative settings API.
- Desktop-only bridge design so Obsidian does not need Full Disk Access.

## Known limitations

- Requires the native Markway macOS app to be installed and configured.
- Full bidirectional attachment editing is not complete yet.
- Apple Journal may not refresh an already-open entry until the entry is reopened.
- Mobile Obsidian is not supported because Apple Journal’s local database is not accessible from iOS apps.
